titleStmt.title: Letter from Bishop Michael Fogarty to John Francis Hogan, 21 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Michael Fogarty

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Bishop Michael Fogarty (1859-1955) to John Francis Hogan (1858-1918). Michael Fogarty was born in Kilcolman, County Cork in 1859. He was ordained in 1885 and in 1904 became Bishop of Killaloe, holding this office until his death in 1955. John Francis Hogan, born in County Clare, was a priest, author and President of St Patrick's College, Maynooth. Fogarty is writing to Hogan in connection with W.Ryan's ordination.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
19/38 Ennis. 21.4.16 My dear Monsignor, A whole lot of my
priests have got knocked
out of action by sickness
so I must ordain. W.
Ryan. 4thDivine and
I hope you will let him
be ready to be ordained
here on Sunday
30th april — next
Sunday week - & let
me know if you please
if that will be all
right.
I am sorry to hear
the accounting which reach
me about Fr. Valen?
Donovan's health are
of the more
alarming character
His heart and liver are in
a bad way and his whole
body dropsica I and
the doctors in
attendance are rather
despondent about
his care,
With good wishes,
Yours sincerely,
M. Fogarty
